Apple leaf.symptoms: No visible signs of disease. Vibrant green leaves and firm stems.
Apple leaf.prevention: Continue regular watering, weeding, and balanced fertilization.
Apple leaf.recommendation: Monitor regularly for any changes. Maintain good soil health.

Apple rust leaf.symptoms: Bright orange-yellow spots on leaves. Small tube-like structures on the underside of leaves.
Apple rust leaf.prevention: Remove nearby cedar trees if possible. Plant rust-resistant varieties.
Apple rust leaf.recommendation: Apply fungicides when orange galls appear on cedar trees in spring.

Apple Scab Leaf.symptoms: Olive-green to brown spots on leaves and fruit. Spots can become dark and velvety.
Apple Scab Leaf.prevention: Remove fallen leaves in autumn. Prune trees to improve air circulation.
Apple Scab Leaf.recommendation: Apply sulfur or copper-based fungicides in early spring.

Corn leaf blight.symptoms: Long, elliptical, grayish-green or tan lesions on leaves.
Corn leaf blight.prevention: Rotate with non-host crops. Use resistant hybrids.
Corn leaf blight.recommendation: Apply foliar fungicides if lesions appear before silking.

Corn rust leaf.symptoms: Small, brownish-red pustules on both upper and lower leaf surfaces.
Corn rust leaf.recommendation: Fungicides are rarely needed unless infection is severe and occurs early.

Potato leaf early blight.symptoms: Small, dark, papery spots on older leaves. Spots often have concentric rings (target-like).
Potato leaf early blight.prevention: Rotate crops. Provide adequate nutrition (especially nitrogen).
Potato leaf early blight.recommendation: Apply fungicides like chlorothalonil or mancozeb when first spots appear.

Potato leaf late blight.symptoms: Dark, water-soaked patches on leaves that turn brown/black. White mold may appear under leaves.
Potato leaf late blight.prevention: Use certified disease-free seed potatoes. Eliminate cull piles.
Potato leaf late blight.recommendation: Immediate application of systemic fungicides. Remove and destroy infected plants.

Tomato leaf.symptoms: No visible signs of disease. Vibrant green leaves and firm stems.
Tomato leaf.prevention: Continue regular watering and balanced fertilization.

Tomato Early blight leaf.symptoms: Brown spots with concentric rings on lower leaves. Yellowing around the spots.
Tomato Early blight leaf.prevention: Stake plants for better airflow. Mulch around the base of plants.
Tomato Early blight leaf.recommendation: Use fungicides like copper or chlorothalonil. Remove lower infected leaves.

Tomato leaf late blight.symptoms: Large, dark brown, water-soaked patches on leaves and stems. Fruit develops firm, brown spots.
Tomato leaf late blight.prevention: Avoid planting near potatoes. Choose resistant varieties.
Tomato leaf late blight.recommendation: Apply fungicides immediately. Remove and destroy infected plants to prevent spread.

Tomato leaf yellow virus.symptoms: New leaves are small, yellow, and curled upward. Plant growth is severely stunted.
Tomato leaf yellow virus.prevention: Control whiteflies using reflective mulches or insecticidal soaps. Use resistant varieties.
Tomato leaf yellow virus.recommendation: Remove and destroy infected plants. There is no cure once infected.

Tomato leaf bacterial spot.symptoms: Small, water-soaked, greasy spots on leaves and fruit. Spots turn dark brown.
Tomato leaf bacterial spot.prevention: Use pathogen-free seeds and transplants. Avoid overhead irrigation.
Tomato leaf bacterial spot.recommendation: Apply copper-based bactericides early in the season.
